narrative:
A front moved through from the west Sunday night. We are in a cooler NW flow today and Tuesday. Humidity is much lower. We have two refreshing days ahead. The only other item of note is a breezy NW wind today. Wednesday will be warmer. The next potential for showers and thundershowers is Thursday afternoon as the atmosphere warms and moistens ahead of the next front.

It's the pre-summer solstice edition of the Monday Message, and we talk El Niño, as well our our next rainfall opporturnites conming up in the Thursday-Friday time frame.

The only other item of note is a breezy NW wind today. Wednesday will be warmer. The next potential for showers and thundershowers is Thursday afternoon as the atmosphere warms and moistens ahead of the next front.

Forecast Discussion

A front moved through Sunday night. Today, the front stalls to our south, and high pressure centered in the Mississippi River Valley gradually takes control. We'll have a cooler, less humid, breezy NW flow.

High pressure settles into the Appalachians briefly Tuesday, keeping us cooler than average for mid-June.

Last night's front returns as a warm front Tuesday night, leading to a warmer Wednesday. The potential for a renegade shower is not zero Tuesday and Wednesday afternoons, but it's too small to mention in the forecast boxes above.

The next front from the west arrives early Friday morning. Thursday is warmer and more humid ahead of the front. Scattered showers and thundershowers develop in the afternoon and remain in play overnight. Detailed timing is difficult for Friday, but the middle of guidance has showers Friday, tapering off in the afternoon.

Cooler air returns Friday night into Saturday. Again, humidity levels decrease, making Saturday a very refreshing day. By Sunday, we are in a return flow with warmer air arriving.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Latest Insights
Monday Message for June 15, 2026

It's the pre-summer solstice edition of the Monday Message, available for subscribers. El Niño has officially arrived, and will have significant implications for our weather heading into fall and winter.

Jun 15, 2026
El Niño Has Officially Arrived

El Niño has officially arrived, as described in the latest Climate Prediction Center ENSO discussion.

This will have significant implications on fall and winter temperature and precipitation in the Southeastern United States. While our region continues to experience significant drought conditions, above-normal precipitation will ultimately make a comeback during fall and winter.

Jun 14, 2026
Monday Message for June 8, 2026

This week's Monday Message highlights a week of hit-or-miss showers and thundershowers that will be returning to the region in typical June fashion. Also, we discuss the movie "Pressure", in theaters now.
